name: PlugIn
summary: A general purpose utility plug-in that can contain one or more commands.

constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of the PlugIn class

methods.name: CreateCommands
methods.summary: Called right after plug-in is created and is responsible for creating all of the commands in a given plug-in. The base class implementation Constructs an instance of every publicly exported command class in your plug-in's assembly.

params.name: unloaded

methods.name: GetLicense(LicenseBuildType, ValidateProductKeyDelegate, OnLeaseChangedDelegate)
methods.signature: protected bool GetLicense(LicenseBuildType productBuildType, ValidateProductKeyDelegate validateProductKeyDelegate, OnLeaseChangedDelegate leaseChangedDelegate)
methods.summary: Verifies that there is a valid product license for your plug-in, using the Rhino licensing system. If the plug-in is installed as a standalone node, the locally installed license will be validated. If the plug-in is installed as a network node, a loaner license will be requested by the system's assigned Zoo server. If the Zoo server finds and returns a license, then this license will be validated. If no license is found, then the user will be prompted to provide a license key, which will be validated.

params.name: productBuildType
params.doc: The product build contentType required by your plug-in.

params.name: validateProductKeyDelegate
params.doc: Since the Rhino licensing system knows nothing about your product license, you will need to validate the product license provided by the Rhino licensing system. This is done by supplying a callback function, or delegate, that can be called to perform the validation.

params.name: leaseChangedDelegate
params.doc: Called by the ZooClient when the cloud zoo lease is changed.

returns.doc: true if a valid license was found. false otherwise.

methods.name: GetLicense(LicenseCapabilities, String, ValidateProductKeyDelegate, OnLeaseChangedDelegate)
methods.signature: protected bool GetLicense(LicenseCapabilities licenseCapabilities, string textMask, ValidateProductKeyDelegate validateProductKeyDelegate, OnLeaseChangedDelegate leaseChangedDelegate)
methods.summary: Verifies that there is a valid product license for your plug-in, using the Rhino licensing system. If the plug-in is installed as a standalone node, the locally installed license will be validated. If the plug-in is installed as a network node, a loaner license will be requested by the system's assigned Zoo server. If the Zoo server finds and returns a license, then this license will be validated. If no license is found, then the user will be prompted to provide a license key, which will be validated.

params.name: licenseCapabilities
params.doc: In the event that a license was not found, or if the user wants to change the way your plug-in is licenses, then provide what capabilities your license has by using this enumeration flag.

params.name: textMask
params.doc: In the event that the user needs to be asked for a license, then you can provide a text mask, which helps the user to distinguish between proper and improper user input of your license code. Note, if you do not want to use a text mask, then pass in a null value for this parameter. For more information on text masks, search MSDN for the System.Windows.Forms.MaskedTextBox class.

params.name: validateProductKeyDelegate
params.doc: Since the Rhino licensing system knows nothing about your product license, you will need to validate the product license provided by the Rhino licensing system. This is done by supplying a callback function, or delegate, that can be called to perform the validation.

params.name: leaseChangedDelegate
params.doc: Called by the ZooClient when the cloud zoo lease is changed.

returns.doc: true if a valid license was found. false otherwise.

methods.name: LoadPlugIn(String, Guid)

methods.name: ObjectPropertiesPages(ObjectPropertiesPageCollection)
methods.summary: Override this function is you want to extend the object properties dialog. This method will be called each time a new document is created for each instance of a object properties panel. On Windows there will be a single panel per document but on Mac there may be many properties panel per document.

params.doc: Add custom pages by calling collection.Add

returns.doc: An appropriate load return code. The default implementation returns Success.

methods.name: RaiseOnPlugInSettingsSavedEvent
methods.summary: Raise any pending OnPlugInSettingsSaved events, the events are normally queued while a command is running and fired while Rhino is in an idle state. Calling this method will raise any pending changed events regardless of Rhino's current idle state or if a command is running.

methods.name: ReadDocument
methods.signature: protected virtual void ReadDocument(RhinoDoc doc, BinaryArchiveReader archive, FileReadOptions options)
methods.summary: Called whenever a Rhino document is being loaded and plug-in user data was encountered written by a plug-in with this plug-in's GUID.

params.name: doc
params.doc: A Rhino document that is being loaded.

params.name: archive
params.doc: OpenNURBS file archive object Rhino is using to read this file. Use BinaryArchiveReader.Read*() functions to read plug-in data. If any BinaryArchive.Read*() functions throws an exception then archive.ReadErrorOccurve will be true and you should immediately return.

params.name: options
params.doc: Describes what is being written.

methods.name: ShouldCallWriteDocument
methods.summary: Called whenever a Rhino is about to save a .3dm file. If you want to save plug-in document data when a model is saved in a version 5 .3dm file, then you must override this function to return true and you must override WriteDocument().

params.name: options
params.doc: The file write options, such as "include preview image" and "include render meshes".

returns.doc: true if the plug-in wants to save document user data in the version 5 .3dm file. The default returns false.

methods.name: WriteDocument
methods.signature: protected virtual void WriteDocument(RhinoDoc doc, BinaryArchiveWriter archive, FileWriteOptions options)
methods.summary: Called when Rhino is saving a .3dm file to allow the plug-in to save document user data.

params.name: doc
params.doc: The Rhino document instance that is being saved.

params.name: archive
params.doc: OpenNURBS file archive object Rhino is using to write the file. Use BinaryArchiveWriter.Write*() functions to write plug-in data. OR use the ArchivableDictionary If any BinaryArchiveWriter.Write*() functions throw an exception, then archive.WriteErrorOccured will be true and you should immediately return. Setting archive.WriteErrorOccured to true will cause Rhino to stop saving the file.

params.name: options
params.doc: The file write options, such as "include preview image" and "include render meshes".

properties.name: AskOnLoadProtection
properties.summary: If true, Rhino will display a warning dialog when load-protected plug-ins are attempting to load. If false, load-protected plug-ins will silently not load.

properties.name: Description
properties.summary: Returns the description of the plug-in, as found in the plug-in's assembly attributes.

properties.name: Id
properties.summary: Returns the Guid, or unique Id, of the plug-in.

properties.name: InstalledPlugInCount
properties.summary: Returns the number of installed Rhino plug-ins.

properties.name: LoadTime
properties.summary: Plug-ins are typically loaded on demand when they are first needed. You can change this behavior to load the plug-in at during different stages in time by overriding this property.

properties.name: LocalPlugInName
properties.summary: Optionally override this to provide a localized plug-in name

properties.name: Name
properties.summary: Returns the name of the plug-in, as found in the plug-in's assembly attributes.

properties.name: SettingsDirectory
properties.summary: Get the plug-in's settings directory. This is the directory where the plug-in's persistent settings files are saved. This directory will be located in the user's profile folder. Note, this does not verify the directory exists.

properties.name: SettingsDirectoryAllUsers
properties.summary: Get the plug-in's "all users" settings directory. This directory will be located in the system's program data folder. Note, this does not verify the directory exists.

properties.name: Version
properties.summary: Returns the version of the plug-in, as found in the plug-in's assembly attributes.

events.name: SettingsSaved
events.summary: This event is raised when an instance of Rhino has modified the external settings file associated with this plug-in's Settings property.

events.name: UnknownUserData
events.summary: This event is raised when a 3dm file is loaded with unknown user data, and the plug-in to handle it could not be loaded.
